subject: [MULTICLOUD] Service mesh compatibility
commit_message:
[MULTICLOUD] Service mesh compatibility

Patch on MC charts dedicated to make http/https calls depending
on configuration for TLS. Include an updated version of the
framework-artifactbroker

Issue-ID: OOM-3015

Signed-off-by: Andreas Geissler <andreas-geissler@telekom.de>
Change-Id: I03deae9dcea703540fe6e3a81ddba20505f246f1

Patch Set 19:

The most recent gating run has a very odd result:  there is a Helm deployment error in trying to create the nodePort Service for a1policymanagement-external on port 30294.  (See https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//129266-19/k8s/k8s/onap-helm/helm.html).  There is another service already using the port.  It's onap-strimzi-2.  (See https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//129266-19/k8s/k8s/kubernetes-status/index.html, select the services tab, and search for 30294.)  

For comparison, look at the list of services for today's gating run at https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/daily/onap-daily-dt-oom-master/2022-09/12_05-21/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/kubernetes-status/index.html.  The a1policymanagement-external nodePort service is present on 30294, and the onap-strimzi-2 is on 31342.  There are no Helm deployment errors.

My sense is that the changes here for multicloud are probably not at fault, but I would like to understand (and see a fix for) this apparent nodePort conflict issue.

(Coincidentally--or maybe not--the multicloud project in the past used a nodePort service on port 30294 for a component called multicloud-windriver.  multicloud-windriver is no longer deployed, and it was not deployed in the gating run here.)

Patch Set 19:

Yes, I see these NodePort conflicts appearing from time to time.
The problem seems, that there is a bug in the strimzi chart.
https://git.onap.org/oom/tree/kubernetes/strimzi/templates/strimzi-kafka.yaml#n49
As you can see, it reserves reserves the ports:
30491: onap-strimzi-kafka-external-bootstrap
30492: onap-strimzi-kafka-0
30493: onap-strimzi-kafka-1 
The port for onap-strimzi-kafka-2 is missing and therefor randomly chosen !!
If this is in conflict with any other service, like here a1policymanagement, this fails.
So the simple solution is to add the lines:
            - broker: 2
              nodePort: {{ .Values.global.nodePortPrefixExt }}94

but check, if the port is not already reserved.
I will create a ticket for Strimzi... and provide a fix.

I noticed the issue with the kafka port alright. I increased the replica count to 3 without adding a new node port.
I do need to question if we actually need node ports for the kafka pods as I am not sure if they are needed.
The bootstrap server already has one so I will need to check with possible users of the external ports on the kafka cluster
